name: Firewall rule allows RDP (TCP/3389) from the internet
description: A VPC firewall rule allows inbound TCP traffic on port 3389 (RDP) from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0. This exposes Windows VMs to brute-force attacks, BlueKeep-style RDP vulnerabilities, and remote ransomware deployment. Open RDP is consistently ranked as one of the top initial access methods in cloud incident response.
remediation: Delete or restrict the firewall rule. Use IAP for RDP tunneling instead of exposing port 3389: gcloud compute start-iap-tunnel. If direct RDP is needed, restrict source IPs to specific CIDR ranges and enforce MFA via Cloud Identity.
